title: selaginella schultesii
intro: Selaginella schultesii, also known as the 'Mexican spikemoss' or 'False maidenhair fern', is a type of small, low-growing, evergreen plant that belongs to the Selaginella genus, which is part of the Selaginellaceae family. It is native to Mexico and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and fever, and it also has potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. Selaginella schultesii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal purposes, and it is also sometimes used in traditional herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
